name: Garden of Eden
description: The place where God created Adam and Eve. Its location is described near four rivers including the Tigris and Euphrates.

name: Ur of the Chaldeans
description: Ancient Sumerian city-state in present-day Iraq. Birthplace of Abraham, from which God called him to depart.

name: Haran
description: City where Terah and Abraham settled on their way from Ur. Terah died here. Abraham departed from here at age 75. Jacob fled here and lived with Laban.

name: Land of Canaan
description: The land God promised to Abraham and his descendants. The primary homeland of the patriarchs.

name: Shechem
description: First place Abraham stopped in Canaan. God appeared to him here and promised the land to his descendants.

name: Hebron (Mamre)
description: Abraham settled near Mamre in Hebron. The cave of Machpelah, purchased by Abraham, became the burial site of Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, Rebekah, Leah, and Jacob.

name: Beer-sheba
description: Where Abraham and Abimelech made a covenant. Isaac built an altar here after God appeared to him. Jacob departed from here to Egypt.

name: Bethel
description: Jacob had his dream of the stairway to heaven here and called the place 'Beth-el' (House of God).

name: Dothan
description: Where Joseph's brothers threw him into a pit and sold him to the Ishmaelite traders going to Egypt.

name: Egypt
description: Joseph was brought here as a slave and rose to become second-in-command. Jacob and his family of 70 settled in Goshen during the famine. Moses led the Israelites out of Egypt in the Exodus.

name: Land of Goshen
description: The fertile region in northeastern Egypt where Jacob's family settled at Joseph's invitation. The Israelites lived here for approximately 430 years.

name: Jericho
description: The first Canaanite city conquered by Israel under Joshua. Rahab, an ancestor of Jesus, hid the Israelite spies here and was spared.

name: Moab
description: Where Naomi and her family lived during the famine. Ruth, a Moabite woman, chose to follow Naomi back to Israel and became an ancestor of David.

name: Bethlehem
description: Hometown of Boaz and Ruth. Birthplace of David, anointed king here by Samuel. Birthplace of Jesus, fulfilling the prophecy of Micah 5:2.

name: Jerusalem
description: David captured this city and made it his capital. Solomon built the First Temple here. Jesus entered on a donkey, was crucified at Golgotha, and rose from the dead — the culmination of the messianic line.

name: Ramah
description: Home and burial place of Samuel. Where Saul first met Samuel.

name: Babylon
description: The empire that conquered Judah and took the people into exile. Mentioned as the turning point in Matthew 1:11-12 — 'at the time of the deportation to Babylon.'

name: Nazareth
description: Hometown of Joseph and Mary. Where the angel Gabriel announced the birth of Jesus to Mary. Jesus grew up here.
